---
title: "EWG 3.1: Agenda & Meeting Resources"
format: html
---

<div class="button-container">
  <a href="ewg31_agenda_spanish.html" class="custom-button">
    <i class="fas fa-globe"></i> en español
  </a>
</div>

## Expert Working Group 3.1

**Thursday, July 16, 2026 | 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM (PST)**

Welcome to EWG 3.1! This summer, we are presenting and discussing final results for the Gulf of California Ocean Health Index at a rate of roughly three goals per meeting. Our objectives for each meeting are to clearly explain the goal model, flag regional scores that do not make sense, and review any major concerns you might have, as opposed to discussing the finer details or data layers associated with each model.

---

::: {.callout-tip}
# Key links

| Resource | Link |
|----------|------|
| **Meeting Agenda** | [OHI GOC EWG 3.1 Agenda (July 16)](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1huRjetwhfuGDyfVCr7x82a6lVxe_yty3F3cnnQ_rQks/edit?gid=0#gid=0) |
| **Attendance & Notes** | [OHI GOC EWG 3.1 Attendance & Notes](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wUw9c7JtcpR4tIUv2m4Dog6MhVmzD53d4jmzi078G4Q/edit?tab=t.nfspcrfad5zn#heading=h.hiwxizsktkp4) |
| **OHI Introduction Info Sheet (English)** | [OHI GoC: Introduction Info Sheet](https://docs.google.com/document/d/17Kiwk6Qelq_ph81plGCgIMDlZGAlS1b_0N9AvHZqE9Y/edit?tab=t.0) |
| **OHI Introduction Info Sheet (Spanish)** | [OHI GoC: Hoja informativa de introducción](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1GhLuecC2LLVxrOca8TqnjuOCXb5NHgfxX0oCVBeyH7s/edit?tab=t.0) |
:::

---

## July 16 meeting schedule

Each goal receives a **20 minute presentation** followed by **40 minutes for discussion and questions**. We aim for a **10 minute break** between each goal.

| Time | Agenda item |
|------|-------------|
| 9:00 to 9:25 AM | Getting started. Big picture overview of project, meeting objectives, and resources. |
| 9:25 to 10:25 AM | **Goal Discussion: Tourism & Recreation** (20 min presentation, 40 min discussion) |
| 10:25 to 10:35 AM | Break |
| 10:35 to 11:35 AM | **Goal Discussion: Food Provisioning** (20 min presentation, 40 min discussion) |
| 11:35 to 11:45 AM | Break |
| 11:45 AM to 12:45 PM | **Goal Discussion: Livelihoods & Economies** (20 min presentation, 40 min discussion) |
| 12:45 to 1:00 PM | Wrap up and next steps |

---

## Goals for this summer's EWG meetings

### EWG 3.1 (July 16, 2026)

**Goals under review:** Tourism & Recreation, Food Provisioning, Livelihoods & Economies

**Tourism & Recreation (TR)** captures the quantity, quality, and sustainability of experiences that people have when visiting coastal and marine areas. It focuses on the personal and cultural value of coastal recreation, not on revenue or livelihoods generated by tourism (those are covered under Livelihoods & Economies). The TR model combines three components: tourist arrivals (quantity), safety and availability of activities (quality), and certifications, job quality, and tourism management investment (sustainability).

**Food Provisioning (FP)** measures the amount of seafood that is sustainably produced by wild catch fisheries and mariculture operations in the Gulf of California. Materials for this goal will be added to the website as they become available.

**Livelihoods & Economies (LE)** measures jobs and revenue from sustainable marine related industries. It has two subgoals: Livelihoods (quantity and quality of marine related jobs) and Economies (revenue from marine related sectors). The model uses INEGI Censos Económicos data to track employment, job quality, and sector revenue across the nine OHI regions.

### EWG 3.2 (August 13, 2026)

**Goals under review (subject to change):** Habitat Services, Access Opportunities, Sense of Place

**Habitat Services** (consolidated from Carbon Storage and Coastal Protection) captures the ability of coastal habitats to buffer against climate change by sequestering carbon and to protect coastal areas from flooding and erosion.

**Access Opportunities** evaluates whether members of coastal communities, including small scale fishing communities and pueblos originarios, have legal and regulated access to marine resources that allow them to thrive.

**Sense of Place** assesses the deep sense of identity and belonging provided through connections with marine communities, including iconic species and lasting special places.

### EWG 3.3 (September 10, 2026)

**Goals under review (subject to change):** Biodiversity, Clean Waters, Natural Products, Safety & Security*

*Biodiversity* assesses the condition of local habitats and biodiversity as proxies for functionality of marine ecosystems. *Clean Waters* evaluates the condition of marine waters. *Natural Products* quantifies benefits from sustainable harvest of commercially and culturally important non food marine resources. *Safety & Security* is technically not a goal but will act as a pressure on all goals, reflecting how safety concerns affect people's ability to benefit from ocean resources.

---

## Website resources for EWG 3.1 goals

| Goal | Website page |
|------|--------------|
| Tourism & Recreation | [View TR model and visualizations](ewg31_tr.html) |
| Food Provisioning | [View FP page](ewg31_fp.html) *(materials forthcoming)* |
| Livelihoods & Economies | [View LE model and visualizations](ewg31_le.html) |
